images.caption:
Figure 1:Switchoverview.(a) Training.Three phases turn a Qwen3 base into a
switchable latent reasoner: SFT to wrap high-entropy CoT spans
in<swi>/</swi>, a curriculum that gradually replaces text
inside those spans with<latent>positions (jointly,Switch-SFT), and Switch-GRPO for on-policy RL on the
answer reward.(b1) Inference token stream.The model emits<swi>to
enter latent mode, runs a block of<latent>steps, and emits</swi>to resume text decoding.(b2) Hidden-state recurrence inside the block.Each
latent step’s last-layer hidden state becomes the input
embedding of the next<latent>position (Coconut-style
recurrence).

Figure 2:Sequential vs. parallel curriculum
schedules.Hidden states (green circles) replace
text inside<swi>-spans either one span at a time or in every
span simultaneously across curriculum stages.

Figure 3:Switch-GRPO training trajectory, four metrics
overlaid with per-metric min–max normalisation. Legend shows
the actual start→\toend values; the dashed line marks the
reported step-800800checkpoint.

Figure 4:Accuracy–efficiency operating curveon the
representative training run. MATH-500 accuracy vs. average
visible tokens. The Switch-GRPO endpoint (star) sits at the
high-accuracy end; the brevity-bonus operating point (diamond)
trades a modest amount of accuracy for shorter outputs and zero
max-length truncation. Dashed curve: empirical Pareto frontier.

Figure 5:Distribution of visible tokens per problem,SFT vs. Switch-GRPO vs. +brevity. Dashed lines mark each
checkpoint’s mean.

Figure 6:Switch-window curves.p​(<swi>)p(\texttt{<swi>})(solid, left
log axis) and entropy (dashed, right linear axis) at relative
offsets−8,…,+8-8,\ldots,+8around annotated<swi>positions. The
spike at the boundary collapses by several orders of magnitude
one token later. Switch-GRPO preserves the spike but softens
its peak height; per-offset values at−1,0,+1-1,0,+1in
Table4.

Figure 7:Linear probe accuracyfor “next token is<swi>” at seven layer offsets. Both checkpoints rise from near
chance in the early layers to≥88%\geq\!88\%at the last layer
(exact numbers in Table5).

Figure 8:Latent-state intervention.Accuracy (left) and
answer-change rate (right) under four intervention modes on the
unrestricted MATH-500 set (light bars) and on the diagnostic
subset (dark bars).Zerocollapses diagnostic accuracy
from100%100\%to33%33\%(−66.7-66.7points);Random-normandSkipare far less destructive. The latent
computation is the specific hidden state of Eq.1,
not just any non-zero perturbation.

Figure 9:Full Switch-GRPO trajectory(1,9641{,}964optimizer
steps) on the representative training run. Light scatter is raw
per-batch metrics; coloured lines are8080-step rolling means.
The pink-shaded region marks the late-training reward-hacking
regime (≳step​1,200\gtrsim\!\text{step}\ 1{,}200); the dashed vertical line
marks the reported step-800800checkpoint, our early-stopping point.
The main-body Fig.3shows the same panels
cropped to the stable0–1,0001{,}000window.
Figure 10:Empirical CDF of visible tokens per problem.

Figure 11:Per-subject (left) and per-difficulty (right)
MATH-500 accuracyofSwitchafter Switch-GRPO.
